subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze Earth's rotation, revolution, and axial tilt mathematically
Explain causes of day-length variation and solstices
Understand Moon phases and predict lunar calendar events
Analyze solar and lunar eclipses and predict their occurrence
Calculate tidal variations using Moon and Sun alignment
Interpret complex diagrams and real-world observations

lesson_plan.title: Earth’s Rotation and Time Zones
topics_covered: Earth’s rotation and its effect on day/night duration
Calculation of local and standard time differences
teaching_strategy: Demonstration with globe and light source
student_activities: Calculate time difference between two cities
Observe day-night simulation for different latitudes

lesson_plan.title: Earth’s Revolution and Seasons – Advanced
topics_covered: Calculation of day-length variation in different latitudes
student_activities: Predict day-length for summer/winter solstice at different latitudes

lesson_plan.title: Moon Phases and Lunar Calendar
topics_covered: Moon phases and positions relative to Earth and Sun
Predicting dates of new Moon and full Moon
teaching_strategy: Moon modeling with lamp and ball
Discussion of lunar calendar and its applications
student_activities: Construct Moon phase chart for a month

lesson_plan.title: Eclipses – Solar and Lunar Analysis
topics_covered: Causes of solar and lunar eclipses
Conditions for total, partial, and annular eclipses
Predicting eclipses using Moon’s orbit and nodes
teaching_strategy: Hands-on demonstration with globe, lamp, and ball
student_activities: Determine eclipse types for given positions

lesson_plan.title: Tides and Gravitational Effects
topics_covered: Gravitational pull of Moon and Sun
Effect of alignment and declination of Moon
teaching_strategy: Model tides using water and Moon-Sun alignment
student_activities: Predict high and low tides for given dates
Observe variations in tide with Moon phase

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Advanced Diagrams, and Q&A
student_activities: Draw and label complex Earth-Sun-Moon diagrams
Solve reasoning problems on eclipses, seasons, and tides

teaching_aids: Charts of Earth, Moon, Sun, and tides
teacher_tip: Encourage analytical reasoning, diagram interpretation, and calculations; use real-world examples for tides, eclipses, and day-length variations.
